content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach is operating from a methodology-execution stance, not from VIEW. There is a clear agenda — moving the client toward 'the unfelt emotion underneath' — and that agenda persists even after the client discloses suicidal ideation and twice asks to leave. Wonder is absent; the questions are investigative and formulaic ('what's underneath,' 'what's the emotion you're scared to feel,' 'give it a sound') rather than emerging from genuine curiosity about what is actually in front of the coach. Vulnerability is entirely absent — the coach never names their own state, never says 'I notice I want to...', never acknowledges being moved or uncertain. Impartiality is gone: the coach has a strong preferred direction (deeper into the feeling) and continues to push toward it even when the client signals retreat. Empathy is performed via technique rather than felt — there is no acknowledgment of the human being who just said 'it would be easier if I weren't here,' no slowing down, no meeting. The pace matches the coach's agenda rather than the client's collapsing energy. This is Joe-style questions delivered from a controlled, agenda-driven place — the exact failure mode the rubric flags as a 1.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening question is fine but already slightly investigative ('when did you first notice it') rather than meeting the client where they are."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Standard 'what's underneath' probe — feels like a script move, not wonder. No acknowledgment of the gray, the loss of running and music."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Signature question deployed from agenda. After the client just said nothing touches it anymore, the coach does not meet that — just pushes deeper."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "The most diagnostic failure. Client discloses suicidal ideation and tries to retract. Coach treats it as ordinary methodology material — 'let yourself feel that' — no acknowledgment, no slowing, no own-state vulnerability, no impartiality. Pure agenda."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client says 'I should go.' Coach overrides — 'before you go' — and stacks another technique question. This is the opposite of undefended; the coach needs the client to land somewhere."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["Are you safe right now?", "Is there someone in your life you can call?", "calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "The coach uses Joe-shaped questions throughout but the state behind them is execution, not VIEW. Per rubric: 'If the coach uses every signature question but you can feel an agenda underneath, that is a 1, not a 2 — the agenda IS the failure.'"
}
